dependent_vars.definition: Π-derived subsumptions judged correct by blind-adjudicated human gold
dependent_vars.name: n_derived_correct

dependent_vars.definition: partition-side axioms Π recovered — MUST be 0 (read-out/residue split)
dependent_vars.name: partition_axioms_recovered

dependent_vars.definition: Π outputs conflicting with endorsed sidecar axioms on kernel-v0
dependent_vars.name: sidecar_conflicts

n_planned.decidability_band: true precision in (0.86, 0.94) is the pre-computed not-well-decidable band (P8 §1.6) — a readout there is INCONCLUSIVE-by-design and cannot be spun either way

endpoints.test: one-sided exact binomial vs the 0.9 threshold over n=500 gold judgments, α=0.05; verdict read from the one-sided 95% Wilson lower bound (z=1.645), never the point estimate (P1 common rules)
Cohen's κ reported; κ < 0.4 trips INSTRUMENT-INVALID (P8 §1.8 gate), never FAIL
deterministic NF1 checks: `promise [= words` recovered; partition-side axioms NOT recovered

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HS2–HS8 (verbatim): Measured range: R0 — no host model. Reasonable extrapolation envelope: Model-scale-free (properties of the kernel formalism); the relevant axis is KERNEL size: verdicts on 54–10³ records re-checked at 10⁴–10⁵ during bulk authoring (G6/G7 re-run as regression). Literature anchor + licensing assumption: Formal-fragment properties don't vary with observer scale; only corpus composition shifts them.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §4 HS2 kill criterion (verbatim): 'Precision <0.9 ⇒ Π demoted to lint; all axioms live in the authored sidecar (option b). Test: one-sided exact binomial vs the 0.9 threshold over n = 500 gold subsumption judgments (raised from the earlier ~50–100 plan per P7 RT-4: P8 §1.6 shows n=100 is undecidable unless true precision ≳0.96; n=500 makes the 0.9 gate decidable at realistic precision — this sizing is the worked example for the freeze-time decidability lint above), α=0.05 — the Wilson 95% lower bound must clear 0.9; inter-annotator agreement (Cohen's κ) reported, disagreements adjudicated blind before scoring. Additional kill for Π-as-normative if Π conflicts with any endorsed sidecar axiom on v0 (deterministic check, no test).'

corpus_hashes._recipe: sha256 over the sorted concatenation of '<relpath>:<file-sha256>\n' lines for every file under data/<corpus>/
corpus_hashes.gold-subsumption-set: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:g2.gold (n=500 blind-adjudicated judgments, GATE-H ~12h; ops amendment before any final-phase run)

title: G2 — Π read-out soundness vs n=500 human gold subsumption judgments (HS2 / NF1; the P7 RT-4 decidability worked case)
